description: Diagnosis, risk stratification (RMI I) and management of ovarian cysts in postmenopausal women.

sba.stem: A 58-year-old woman has an incidental 2.4 cm simple, unilateral, unilocular cyst on the right ovary. She is asymptomatic and the rest of the scan is unremarkable.
sba.lead: What is the most appropriate NEXT step?
options: Refer for CA125 and RMI calculation
Refer to the gynaecological oncology MDT
sba.rationale: Simple, unilateral, unilocular cysts of 3 cm or less in a postmenopausal woman are clinically inconsequential and need no follow-up (RCOG GTG 34). The 4-6 month review is reserved for cysts greater than 3 cm and up to 5 cm.

sba.stem: A 61-year-old woman has an asymptomatic 4.2 cm simple, unilateral, unilocular ovarian cyst on transvaginal scan. Serum CA125 is 18 IU/mL.
sba.lead: What is the most appropriate management?
options: Conservative review with scan and CA125 at 4-6 months
Conservative review with scan and CA125 at 12 months
Urgent CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis
sba.rationale: Cysts more than 3 cm but 5 cm or less with a normal CA125 can be managed conservatively with reassessment of CA125 and ultrasound at 4-6 months. Discharge is reserved until 1 year of confirmed stability.
sba.hook: 3-5 cm + normal CA125 -> reassess at 4-6 months.

sba.stem: A 67-year-old woman presents with bloating and a palpable pelvic mass. Ultrasound shows a 9 cm cyst with a solid mural nodule and free fluid in the pouch of Douglas. CA125 is 210 IU/mL.
sba.lead: What is the most appropriate NEXT investigation?
options: CT scan of the abdomen and pelvis
sba.rationale: An RMI I above 200 with suspicious morphology warrants CT of the abdomen and pelvis to assess disease extent, with referral to the gynae-oncology MDT. MRI is reserved as a second-line tool for indeterminate masses, not staging.
sba.hook: RMI >=200 -> CT abdomen and pelvis, MDT.

sba.stem: A 59-year-old woman has an ultrasound score of 2 (multilocular cyst and ascites), and CA125 of 60 IU/mL.
sba.lead: What is the calculated RMI I score?
sba.rationale: RMI I = U x M x CA125. An ultrasound score of 2-5 scores U = 3, menopausal status M = 3, CA125 = 60: RMI = 3 x 3 x 60 = 540. The 180 option is the trap for candidates who use U = 1.
sba.hook: Score 2-5 -> U=3; RMI = U x M x CA125.

sba.stem: A 70-year-old woman with an RMI I of 90 for a 6 cm simple cyst opts for surgery and is fit for laparoscopy.
sba.lead: What is the most appropriate surgical procedure?
sba.rationale: Laparoscopic management of a low-risk postmenopausal cyst should comprise bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy with the specimen removed intact, not cystectomy, to avoid spillage should malignancy be confirmed.
sba.hook: Low-risk postmenopausal cyst -> BSO, not cystectomy.

emq.theme: Initial management of postmenopausal ovarian cysts by size and CA125
emq.instruction: For each woman, select the SINGLE most appropriate management. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.
options: B. Repeat CA125 and ultrasound in 4 to 6 months
C. Discharge from follow-up after 1 year if stable
D. CT scan of abdomen and pelvis
E. Referral to gynaecological oncology MDT
F. MRI pelvis as second-line imaging
H. Aspiration for symptom control only

stems.stem: A 60-year-old woman with an incidental, simple, unilocular 2.1 cm cyst.
stems.rationale: Simple unilocular cysts of 3 cm or less need no follow-up; a 4-6 month scan applies only above 3 cm.

stems.stem: A 62-year-old woman with a 4.5 cm simple unilocular cyst and normal CA125.
stems.rationale: Cysts of 3-5 cm with normal CA125 are managed with repeat ultrasound and CA125 at 4-6 months.

stems.stem: A 71-year-old woman with a complex 8 cm cyst, papillary projections and ascites, CA125 340.
stems.rationale: A high RMI with complex morphology and ascites mandates referral to the gynae-oncology MDT after CT staging.
stems.hook: High RMI + complex features -> MDT referral.

emq.theme: Risk of malignancy index (RMI I) calculation
emq.instruction: For each woman, calculate the SINGLE correct RMI I using RMI = U x M x CA125, where M = 3 for all postmenopausal women. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

stems.stem: Ultrasound score 0, none of the five suspicious features, CA125 45.
stems.rationale: U = 0 gives RMI = 0 x 3 x 45 = 0 regardless of CA125.

stems.stem: Ultrasound score 1, exactly one suspicious feature, CA125 30.
Ultrasound score 3, three suspicious features, CA125 50.
stems.rationale: A score of 2-5 scores U = 3, so RMI = 3 x 3 x 50 = 450.
